title: Tryde Console Table
summary: A long, narrow entryway table with 4x4 legs, a breadboard-ended 2x6 top, and a full apron-and-stretcher base built entirely with pocket holes.
description:
The tall sibling in the "Tryde" table family — same breadboard-end top and pocket-hole joinery as the Tryed Side Table, stretched out to console-table length and height with a proper apron-and-leg base plus a floating center stretcher for rigidity. Build the two leg assemblies as their own sub-units first (short aprons sandwiched between posts), then join them with the long side aprons before the top ever comes into play — trying to square up all four legs at once, freehand, is how a long narrow table ends up racked.

The center stretcher sits loose inside the leg assembly and is added last, after the base is already square — forcing it in earlier can spread the legs out of true. A right-angle drill attachment on your driver makes the tight-clearance screws under the apron much easier to reach; a standard drill/driver works too, just at a more awkward angle.

tools.note: A right-angle attachment helps reach screws under the apron, but a standard drill/driver works.

materials.name: Pine, 2x6, 12 ft
materials.note: Can be cut into a 4 ft and 8 ft piece for easier transport.

cutList.note: Measure and cut to fit as you build for a perfect match.
4 form the leg sets' end aprons; 2 support the lower stretcher.

steps.title: Join the tabletop boards
steps.body: Cut the tabletop boards and drill a 1-1/2" pocket hole on each end. On two of the 2x6 boards, also drill 1-1/2" pocket holes along one long edge. Attach all three boards together with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the breadboard ends
steps.body: Measure and cut the 2x4 breadboard ends, then attach the joined 2x6 center panel to them with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Build the two leg assemblies
steps.body: Drill 1-1/2" pocket holes on each end of four of the short 2x4 pieces, plus pocket holes facing upward on two of them for attaching the tabletop later. Drill pocket holes on each end of the remaining two short pieces for the lower stretcher supports. Build two leg sets by attaching the short 2x4 pieces between pairs of posts with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Join the leg assemblies with the side aprons
steps.body: Drill 1-1/2" pocket holes on each end of the side aprons, plus pocket holes facing upward for attaching the tabletop. Attach the two leg assemblies together with the side aprons using 2-1/2" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Attach the base to the tabletop
steps.body: Attach the base to the underside of the tabletop through the predrilled apron pocket holes with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Build the stretcher
steps.body: Build the stretcher with 2-1/2" screws, predrilling so the screw heads sit below the surface of the wood.

steps.title: Install the stretcher and finish
steps.body: Place the stretcher inside the leg assembly and attach with a couple of 2-1/2" screws. Sand everything smooth and finish with stain and top coat.

images.alt: A long entryway console table with 4x4 legs and a breadboard-ended top
